stories.title: Trump says Iran deal reopening Strait of Hormuz 'largely negotiated,' will be announced soon
stories.summary: A fragile ceasefire has been in place since April 8, punctuated by skirmishes as the U.S. and Iran jockey over the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Trump Says He’ll Announce Negotiated Deal With Iran Shortly
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said that a peace deal with Iran has been “largely negotiated” and he plans to announce an agreement shortly that would reopen the strategic Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: South-east Asian economies struggle to counter energy shock
stories.summary: Iran war leaves oil-poor nations trying to shore up countermeasures while battling inflation

stories.title: Russia pounds Kyiv with missiles and drones in heavy overnight assault
stories.summary: Capital is rocked by explosions after Ukraine’s Zelenskyy warned of an imminent large-scale attack

stories.title: Ukraine Says It’s Under Attack From Major Russian Air Strikes
stories.summary: Kyiv came under a massive Russian drone and missile attack early Sunday, Ukrainian authorities said, hours after President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and the US embassy warned of an imminent strike.

stories.title: Ebola Spreads Across Congo as Flights Halted, Supplies Run Low
stories.summary: Congo suspended flights to the eastern city of Bunia and regional health ministers warned of escalating cross-border risks from Ebola as the outbreak spread across three provinces and overwhelmed contact-tracing efforts.

stories.title: Trump says deal with Iran to reopen Hormuz ‘largely negotiated’
stories.summary: US president says he will announce agreement to end the war after talks with Gulf leaders and allies

stories.title: Hyperscaler Debt Flood Brings Derivatives Bonanza
stories.summary: As big tech companies raise hundreds of billions of dollars to fund artificial intelligence investments, Wall Street banks are increasingly finding they have to trade more credit derivatives to keep doing business with the hyperscalers.

stories.title: US and Iran move closer to extending ceasefire by 60 days, say mediators
stories.summary: Terms of the deal include the gradual reopening of the Strait of Hormuz

stories.title: US Reaches Limit of Sanctions Power in Targeting Iran’s Economy
stories.summary: The Trump administration launched its “Economic Fury” campaign to hobble Iran’s economy with sanctions more than a month ago. So far, the country is unbowed.

stories.title: Secret Service Shoots Man Who Fired Gun Near White House
stories.summary: A man was shot by US Secret Service agents near the White House on Saturday after he opened fire with a handgun, according to a statement from the agency.

stories.title: More War-Driven Inflation Seen in Fed’s Favored Gauge
stories.summary: The Federal Reserve’s favored top-line inflation gauge is rapidly approaching 4% as a war-driven spike in energy costs generates unease that price pressures will broaden.

stories.title: Hungary’s New Premier Warns Predecessor Left Budget ‘Skeletons’
stories.summary: Hungary’s new Prime Minister Peter Magyar said he was hoping for economic growth of about 2% this year but put voters on notice that the state of public finances left by his predecessor made it impossible to make firm forecasts.

stories.title: US warns Japan of severe delays in Tomahawk deliveries due to Iran war
stories.summary: Missiles could come two years late as Pentagon works to replenish stocks depleted in Middle East military campaign

stories.title: Delivery Hero reveals Uber takeover bid at €10bn valuation
stories.summary: German food group has also been approached by rival DoorDash, people familiar with the situation said

stories.title: Pointed News Quiz | Retail, Cryptocurrency, Obituaries
stories.summary: David Gura, Christina Ruffini, and Lisa Mateo of “Bloomberg This Weekend” play Pointed! Wager your points, leverage your bets and answer wisely. A new quiz is available to play each week on Bloomberg.com (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: ‘Move-in Ready’ Means Green for This City’s Homebuyers
stories.summary: Berkeley, California, now requires that homes for sale meet an emissions-saving threshold.

stories.title: Nvidia CEO Urges Super Micro to Tighten Up Amid Taiwan Crackdown
stories.summary: Nvidia Corp. Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang urged Super Micro Computer Inc. to tighten up on compliance after Taiwan detained three people this week for allegedly making fraudulent declarations about AI servers made by its US partner.

stories.title: DeepSeek To Make Permanent 75% Discount on Flagship AI Model
stories.summary: DeepSeek said it will make permanent a steep discount on its flagship V4‑Pro model, maintaining prices for developers at a quarter of their original level.

stories.title: SpaceX, OpenAI and Anthropic IPOs set to test limits of AI boom
stories.summary: Elon Musk, Sam Altman and Dario Amodei battle over who can command Wall Street’s deepest pools of capital

stories.title: Anti-Government Protest Kicks Off Serbia Election Campaign
stories.summary: Tens of thousands of people protested in Serbia’s capital against the government, seeking to keep up pressure on President Aleksandar Vucic even after he backed holding an early election in the fall.

stories.title: Bulls push the S&P 500 back near records — here’s what drove the market last week
stories.summary: The combination of higher oil prices and bond market volatility nearly snapped the S&P 500's winning streak.

stories.title: Chanel’s Mega Dividend Brings Owners’ Windfall to $21B
stories.summary: The billionaire family behind Chanel is on track to pocket at least $21 billion from payouts over the past decade, a huge windfall as their brand prospers during a downturn hitting some luxury-good rivals. Angelina Rascouet joined Christina Ruffini and David Gura on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Alberta Premier Stakes Political Future on Canada Secession Vote
stories.summary: Alberta Premier Danielle Smith’s decision to call a referendum that could lead to the energy-rich province separating from Canada has angered both sides of the debate and triggered calls to challenge her leadership.

stories.title: France Bars Israel Minister Ben Gvir Over Treatment of Activists
stories.summary: France banned Israeli National Security Minister Itamar Ben Gvir from entering the country, citing threats and violence against activists who were trying to get aid to Gaza by sea.

stories.title: Will the Dollar Remain King in a Cashless Society?
stories.summary: The dollar has been the world's reserve currency for decades, but it's history goes back centuries – to before the United States was the United States. Brendan Greeley, author of the new book "The Almighty Dollar: 500 Years of the World's Most Powerful Money," joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the currency's history, explain its dominance in global financ

stories.title: US Says Its Blockade Against Iran Has Redirected 100 Vessels
stories.summary: US forces have redirected 100 commercial vessels during its six-week-long blockade of Iran’s ports, Central Command said.

stories.title: US Ebola Aid Plummets 99% Since Last Outbreak
stories.summary: Former Executive Director of the USAID COVID-19 Task Force Jeremy Konyndyk joined Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. Konyndyk explains to hosts David Gura and Christina Ruffini the negative impact of the US withdrawal from the WHO in January and the dismantling of USAID on the public health response. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Pakistan army chief visits Tehran in push for US-Iran deal
stories.summary: Asim Munir joined mediators including Qatar as effort to prevent resumption of full-scale war intensifies

stories.title: US oil producers increase output to capture price surge from Iran war
stories.summary: Drilling expansion follows 40 per cent jump in cost from global supply crunch that has dented president’s approval ratings

stories.title: Iran, US Signal Progress in Peace Talks as Issues Unresolved
stories.summary: Iran said talks on a peace deal with the US focused on ensuring fighting ends on all fronts are progressing and that other key points of contention will be ironed out at a later stage.

stories.title: Charting the Global Economy: Factory Activity Sags on Inflation
stories.summary: The global economy is showing signs of wearing down with inflation pressures persisting during the third month of a war-induced energy crunch.

stories.title: How illegal factories are undermining China’s solar overcapacity crackdown
stories.summary: For nearly two years, China has been waging an intense campaign to eliminate severe overcapacity in the solar industry – an issue that has sparked vicious price wars, undermined domestic firms’ bottom lines, and fuelled protectionist policies overseas. Yet, despite Beijing’s repeated efforts, the problem remains far from resolved. The sector is still plagued by excess capacity, leaving firms acros

stories.title: Odd Lots: Norman Foster on the Struggle to Build Big (Podcast)
stories.summary: Not many people think of designing buildings as an exercise in economics, but the entire process is defined by constraints around resources (both physical and financial), and an iconic building can also have a huge impact on the wealth and development of the area around it. So how do you encourage private developers to consider the public good when designing new projects? And how are some countrie

stories.title: Russia Is Turning the Screws on Armenia Over Its Tilt to Europe
stories.summary: Armenia’s pivot toward the West is coming under increasing pressure from Vladimir Putin’s Russia as the South Caucasus nation prepares for parliamentary elections next month.

stories.title: The new arms race in computing power
stories.summary: Military capability depends increasingly on data centres. Now governments outpaced in AI are looking to experimental technologies

stories.title: How a ‘Cockroach’ Jibe United India’s Disgruntled Jobless Youth
stories.summary: The “Cockroach Janta Party” is one of India’s most viral movements taking aim at the country’s political establishment. Behind the memes and satire is a deeper story about youth unemployment, political frustration and why Gen Z Indians are increasingly speaking up. Bloomberg's Akriti Sharma explains. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Trump’s 3,711 Trades Point to Multiple Stock-Market Strategies
stories.summary: Index-tracking and automated execution appear to be on show in transactions linked to the president.

stories.title: Bangladesh Central Bank Launches $5 Billion Fund to Spur Growth
stories.summary: Bangladesh’s central bank unveiled a 600 billion-taka ($5 billion) package to boost economic growth, revive shuttered factories and support small businesses.

stories.title: Uganda Confirms Three More Ebola Cases as Outbreak Spreads
stories.summary: Uganda confirmed three new Ebola cases, including a health worker and a driver linked to the country’s first known infection, as authorities race to contain the outbreak. Bloomberg News Senior Editor Jason Gale joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Americans are feeling inflation's pinch into the holiday weekend. Here's where prices are rising the most
stories.summary: Prices related to travel, recreation and food saw particularly steep increases, stretching Americans' wallets as they celebrate the unofficial start of summer.

stories.title: US and Iran Remain in Holding Pattern
stories.summary: Bloomberg News White House Correspondent Kate Sullivan joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the most recent updates on Iran, including President Trump canceling his weekend plans to stay in DC. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: ‘Blue dot fever’? What's really behind a tricky summer dynamic for live music
stories.summary: As prices rise everywhere, consumers are choosing to be more intentional with how they're spending on live music and concerts this year.

stories.title: Japan's Akazawa has Brief Talk with China's Wang at APEC
stories.summary: Japanese Trade Minister Ryosei Akazawa and Chinese Commerce Minister Wang Wentao spoke briefly Friday at an Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation dinner in Suzhou, China. The encounter marks the first face-to-face ministerial interaction between the nations since Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s comments on Taiwan strained ties. Akazawa confirmed the meeting at a press conference but declined to disclo

stories.title: Three signs from APEC that the U.S. and China remain far apart on trade
stories.summary: U.S. and Chinese officials have met and spoken publicly about differing priorities since the Trump-Xi summit concluded in Beijing last week.

stories.title: AI wealth must benefit the public, South Korea's deputy PM says amid Samsung labor tensions
stories.summary: South Korea's deputy PM tells CNBC there are concerns as to whether AI could worsen wealth gaps or lead to job losses.

stories.title: Automation and AI: Masters in Business with the CEO of Honeywell
stories.summary: Barry sits down with Vimal Kapur, Chairman and CEO of Honeywell. They discuss the future of Honeywell as well as Kapur's plan to split the company into three separate entities. They also break down how the company uses automation and what to expect from AI. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: ECB Hike May Be Inevitable to Keep Credibility, Stournaras Says
stories.summary: Preserving the European Central Bank’s credibility is a strong argument in favor of an interest-rate increase next month, according to Governing Council member Yannis Stournaras.

stories.title: No one wins a trade war. Or do they?

stories.title: Rep. Amo Discusses DNC Autopsy, War Powers Vote
stories.summary: Gabe Amo, Democratic Representative of Rhode Island, joins Joe Mathieu on Bloomberg's Balance of Power to discuss the DNC's autopsy of the 2024 election as well as the likelihood of Congress passing a war powers resolution, saying the 'long-term solution' is to end the war in Iran. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Zoom’s Anthropic Investment Has Netted the Company $1 Billion
stories.summary: Zoom Communications Inc., the videoconferencing company, has netted about $1 billion on an investment it made in artificial intelligence startup Anthropic PBC in early 2023.

stories.title: Kevin Warsh sworn in as Fed chair as Trump seeks interest rate cuts
stories.summary: Despite President Donald Trump's demands for lower interest rates, markets are betting the Fed will stay on hold through most, if not all, of 2026.

stories.title: Treasury yields are mixed as investors digest Fed chair swearing in, bond market volatility
stories.summary: The volatility in the bond market continued as new Fed chair Kevin Warsh was sworn in on Friday.

stories.title: Anthropic to Close Over $30 Billion Round as Soon as Next Week
stories.summary: Anthropic PBC is set to close its latest round of funding, which may top $30 billion at a valuation above $900 billion, as soon as next week, according to people familiar with the matter, vaulting ahead of rival OpenAI to become the world’s most valuable AI startup.

stories.title: Analyst Upgrades AI Data Center Play On Potential Deals. Stock Crosses Buy Point.

stories.summary: The US president piled major pressure on Kevin Warsh's predecessor to cut interest rates.

stories.summary: Robert Kaplan, Goldman Sachs vice chair and former president of the Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas, expects Kevin Warsh to be an "excellent" Fed chair. stories.title: Gilt relief rally sends yields to biggest weekly drop since 2023
stories.summary: Burnham pledge to stick to fiscal rules and pullback from bets on higher BoE interest rates drive rebound

stories.title: Are Fintech Apps Already Dead?
stories.summary: Is it the end of fintech and investing apps as we know it? Atomic Invest CEO David Dindi joins Bloomberg Open Interest to talk about the future of finance and why it belongs to AI companions, not apps. He says investing apps could disappear within a decade as AI assistants manage portfolios for consumers. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Surge in 'risk-free' treasury yields sends bond investors in search of better opportunities
stories.summary: Treasury yield surge shows bond market is not 'risk free' after all, but there's opportunity for fixed-income investors in intermediates, BBBs and high yield.

stories.summary: While Jensen Huang said the chip giant had "conceded" the China market, Nvidia reported another blockbuster quarter on Wednesday.

stories.title: New Zealand to Invest in Drones, Fleet to Shield Maritime Routes
stories.summary: New Zealand intends to spend about NZ$1.6 billion ($936 million) on drones, ship maintenance and naval upgrades to bolster the island nation’s maritime security at a time of increasing concern about supply routes.

stories.title: Disney's 'Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu' tallies lowest Thursday preview sales in franchise history
stories.summary: Disney's "Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu" tallied $12 million in Thursday night previews, the lowest collection of advance tickets in franchise history.

stories.title: Two top Walmart executives leave company under new CEO John Furner
stories.summary: Tom Ward, chief operating officer of Walmart's warehouse chain Sam's Club, is retiring, and Cedric Clark, Walmart's EVP of U.S. store operations, is leaving.

stories.title: UK sees hottest day of year as bank holiday travellers face queues
stories.summary: Temperatures hit 28.4C in parts of England on Friday, while delays have been reported at the Port of Dover and Birmingham Airport ahead of the bank holiday weekend.

stories.summary: Borrowing was higher than expected last month and retail sales fell as fuel prices surged.
